results.rendered_html:
vi-√gāh Ā. -gāhate (ep. also ˚ti), to plunge or dive into, bathe in, enter, penetrate, pervade, betake one's self into (acc. or loc.),  RV.  &c. &c.;
to pierce,  AV. ;
to agitate, disturb,  Ragh. ;
to be engrossed by or intent upon, ponder,  BhP. ;
to follow, practise,  Kum. ;
to reach, obtain,  Bhartṛ. ;
to approach, fall (as night),  MBh.  :
Pass. -gāhyate, to be plunged into or penetrated, to be entered into or engaged upon,  Kāv. ;  Pañcat.

results.rendered_html:
vigāh 1 A.
1 To plunge or dive into, bathe; (dīrghikāḥ) sa vyagāhata vigāḍhamanmathaḥ  R. 19. 9. 
2 To enter, penetrate into, pervade (fig. also); viṣamo'pi vigāhyate nayaḥ kṛtatīrthaḥ payasāmivāśayaḥ  Ki. 2. 3 ;  R. 13. 1 ,  Mu. 1. 26. 
3 To stir about, agitate; vigāhyamānāṃ sarayūṃ ca naubhiḥ  R. 14. 30. 
4 To follow, practise; tayā munīnāṃ caritaṃ vyagāhyata  Ku. 5. 19. 
5 To approach, set in (as season &c.).
